title: Lesson 8 — The Negotiation

words.definition: stubbornly refusing to change one's opinion or course of action
words.example: The obstinate mule would not move another inch.

words.definition: stubbornly hardened against persuasion or pity
words.example: The landlord stayed obdurate about the rent increase.

words.definition: unwilling to change one's views or to compromise
words.example: Both sides stayed intransigent, and the talks collapsed.

words.definition: stubbornly resistant to authority or control; unruly
words.example: A recalcitrant student who ignored every rule.

words.definition: hard to control, manage, or solve
words.example: An intractable dispute that dragged on for years.

words.definition: unable to be appeased or calmed; relentless
words.example: An implacable rival who never forgave a slight.

words.definition: refusing to be persuaded; unshakably firm
words.example: She was adamant that the meeting start on time.

words.definition: easy to control, manage, or persuade; docile
words.example: Well trained and tractable, the horse followed every command.
New recruits are malleable, eager to learn the rules.

words.definition: open and responsive to suggestion; willing to agree
words.example: He was amenable to pushing the deadline back a week.

words.definition: inclined to agree with others or obey rules; submissive
words.example: A compliant staff that never questioned an order.

words.definition: to give in or surrender, especially after resistance
words.example: Exhausted, the union finally capitulated on the last point.

story: For weeks the two sides sat across the table, equally [[obstinate|obstinate]]. Management was [[obdurate|obdurate]], the union [[intransigent|intransigent]], and a handful of [[recalcitrant|recalcitrant]] shop stewards made the dispute nearly [[intractable|intractable]]. The company's lawyer was [[implacable|implacable]]; the union chief just as [[adamant|adamant]]. A young mediator wondered whether either party could ever be [[tractable|tractable]]. Slowly she found the [[malleable|malleable]] ones in the room — a few members [[amenable|amenable]] to compromise and one manager quietly [[compliant|compliant]] with head office — until, near 3 a.m., the weaker side agreed to [[capitulate|capitulate]] on a single clause, and the deadlock cracked.
